2606.09351.title: In-Context Learning for the Imputation of Public Opinion Data with Large Language Models
2606.09351.paper_summary: Large language models have been widely evaluated as simulators of individual survey responses. In practice, however, fully unobserved responses are rare; the dominant problem is partial non-response. Imputation aims to restore the overall structure of a survey dataset by filling in these missing values. It has its own well-defined evaluation criteria and differs fundamentally from prediction. We propose to impute missing survey data through in-context learning (ICL). We systematically evaluate ICL design choices across different missingness mechanisms (MCAR, MAR, MNAR) on 150 opinion variables spanning 15 waves of the American Trends Panel. Compared to well-established statistical methods for data imputation like MICE PMM, our ICL approach consistently reduces absolute error across all missingness mechanisms, with the largest gains under non-random missingness (MNAR). Notably, the best-performing specification (gpt-oss-120b with 100 in-context examples) achieves near-nominal aggregate coverage (approaching the 95% level) with confidence intervals two to five times narrower than MICE PMM. We publish a Python package with an sklearn-like API to enable easy deployment of our method using local and proprietary LLMs.
2606.09351.paper_summary_zh: 大型語言模型已被廣泛評估為個別調查回應的模擬器。<lb/>然而，在實踐中，完全未觀察的回應是罕見的；主要問題是部分不回應。<lb/>插補旨在通過填補這些缺失值來恢復調查數據集的整體結構。<lb/>它有自己明確的評估標準，並在根本上與預測不同。<lb/>我們提議通過上下文學習（ICL）來插補缺失的調查數據。<lb/>我們系統地評估了在150個意見變量上，針對不同缺失機制（MCAR、MAR、MNAR）的ICL設計選擇，這些變量橫跨15波的美國趨勢面板。<lb/>與像MICE PMM這樣的成熟統計數據插補方法相比，我們的ICL方法在所有缺失機制下持續降低絕對誤差，尤其在非隨機缺失（MNAR）下獲得了最大的增益。<lb/>值得注意的是，表現最佳的規範（gpt-oss-120b，具有100個上下文示例）實現了接近名義總體覆蓋率（接近95%水平），其置信區間比MICE PMM狹窄兩到五倍。<lb/>我們發布了一個具有sklearn風格API的Python包，以便使用本地和專有的LLM輕鬆部署我們的方法。
2606.09351.author: Tobias Holtdirk et.al.
2606.09351.authors: Tobias Holtdirk, Georg Ahnert, Joseph W Sakshaug, Anna-Carolina Haensch
